resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived MCM6/LCT rs4988235 CT call is available for lactase-persistence context. This is not a diagnosis, symptom explanation, diet recommendation, supplement recommendation, weight-management result, lab interpretation, or model-derived health result.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: This marker can provide lactose-related context, but symptoms, diet history, labs, environment, medications, and professional review matter for health and nutrition decisions.

resultRows.sourceLabel: MedlinePlus DTC result limits / Nutritional genomics review
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: The report can describe supplied marker context and observed sample format, but it should not make weight-loss, diet, supplement, exercise, appetite, lab, metabolic, child-specific, diagnosis, or treatment conclusions without validated evidence.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample preview says the Health Action Plan is customized from DNA test results plus medical history, family medical history, gender, and ethnic background, and that recommendations are selected for actionability. The visible sample pages introduce SNP-based report structure, human-study review criteria, recommendation categories, educational-use limits, and healthcare-provider consultation language.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed public Thrive sample-report structure only. Keep this separate from local genome evidence; it is not personal weight-loss, diet, supplement, exercise, appetite, lab, metabolic, diagnosis, or treatment advice.

resultRows.groupTitle: Dietary Factors / Weight Loss and Low Fat Diets
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample trait text says people with similar genetic markers may see greater weight-loss results when following a low-fat diet and that others may not experience weight-loss results on a low-carbohydrate, high-fat diet. The sample also describes dietary-fat response context and gene definitions for UCP3, IRS1, and TCF7L2.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed public Thrive sample-report structure only. Keep this separate from local genome evidence; it is not personal weight-loss, diet, supplement, exercise, appetite, lab, metabolic, diagnosis, or treatment advice.

resultRows.groupTitle: Dietary Factors / Weight Loss and Low Fat Diets
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: Observed sample cards include reduce dietary fat intake, whole plant-based carbohydrates, increase high-quality protein sources, healthy fat choices, total cholesterol, LDL-C, and triglyceride context. The sample cards include examples of diet, lipid-marker, and practitioner-context output blocks in the report.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed public Thrive sample-report structure only. Keep this separate from local genome evidence; it is not personal weight-loss, diet, supplement, exercise, appetite, lab, metabolic, diagnosis, or treatment advice.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample trait text says people with similar genetic markers may be predisposed to difficulty regulating food consumption or stopping eating, and describes neurotransmitter and food-preference context. The sample page includes MC4R appetite and energy-regulation context and TAS2R38 bitter-taste and food-preference context.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed public Thrive sample-report structure only. Keep this separate from local genome evidence; it is not personal weight-loss, diet, supplement, exercise, appetite, lab, metabolic, diagnosis, or treatment advice.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: Observed sample cards include locking up or tossing out unhealthy snack-like foods, stress-reduction techniques, increasing omega-3 fatty-acid foods, and fasting leptin context. The sample card structure includes behavioral, stress, omega-3 food, and hormone-context output blocks.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed public Thrive sample-report structure only. Keep this separate from local genome evidence; it is not personal weight-loss, diet, supplement, exercise, appetite, lab, metabolic, diagnosis, or treatment advice.

thriveContext: Use this report as educational lifestyle genetics context and sample-format replication, not as child-specific, weight-loss, diet, workout, supplement, lab, or treatment guidance.
Consider lifestyle goals, diet history, activity, symptoms, labs, children, medications, medical history, and professional support when making decisions.
Do not use genotype alone or sample-only rows to prescribe diets, interpret cholesterol, LDL-C, triglycerides, leptin, or change treatment.
